title: Foundation
description: Establish the legal and financial foundation of your business.
recommendedActions: Consult with legal/tax professionals about forming an LLC or Corporation.
Apply for an EIN from the IRS.
Open a dedicated business bank account.
cautionNote: Do not apply for credit until your entity is properly formed and you have an active EIN and business bank account.

steps.title: Get EIN
steps.description: Apply for an Employer Identification Number from the IRS.

steps.title: Open Business Bank Account
steps.description: Set up a dedicated checking account for business expenses.

title: Business Identity
description: Ensure your business appears credible and professional to lenders.
recommendedActions: Set up a professional website and domain-based email.
Ensure your business address and phone number are consistent across all directories.
cautionNote: Inconsistencies in your business name, address, or phone number can lead to automatic credit denials.

steps.title: Set Up Contact Info
steps.description: Get a dedicated business phone number and professional address.

steps.title: Establish Web Presence
steps.description: Create a website and domain email (e.g., info@yourdomain.com).

title: Starter Vendors
description: Begin building your business credit profile with Net-30 accounts.
recommendedActions: Research Tier 1 vendors that report to major business credit bureaus.
Make small purchases and pay invoices early or on time.
cautionNote: Always verify directly with the vendor that they currently report to the bureaus before relying on them for your credit building strategy.

steps.title: Research Tier 1 Vendors
steps.description: Identify Net-30 accounts that match your business needs.

steps.title: Open 3-5 Net-30 Accounts
steps.description: Apply for starter vendor accounts and make initial purchases.

steps.title: Pay Invoices Early
steps.description: Pay all invoices well before the 30-day deadline.

title: Verification
description: Confirm that your payment history is being accurately reported.
recommendedActions: Monitor your business credit reports (D&B, Experian, Equifax).
Verify that your Net-30 accounts are showing up.
cautionNote: It can take 30-90 days for vendor payments to appear on your credit reports.

steps.title: Monitor Credit Reports
steps.description: Check your D&B, Experian, and Equifax business reports.

steps.title: Verify Tradelines
steps.description: Ensure your vendor accounts are reporting correctly.

title: Funding Readiness
recommendedActions: Research Tier 2 and Tier 3 credit options (store cards, fleet cards, cash credit cards).
Ensure your financials and documentation are in order.
Understand the requirements for personal guarantees (PG).
cautionNote: This tool does not guarantee approval. Always review the specific underwriting requirements of any lender before applying.

steps.title: Research Advanced Credit
steps.description: Look into Tier 2/3 credit options suitable for your business.

steps.title: Prepare Financials
steps.description: Ensure your revenue documentation is accurate and organized.
